params.prompt: Photorealistic interior photograph of a medium Relax, outdoor/covered patio area with wide openings to living/dining windows, Outdoors/covered patio: retain natural daylight; use one warm outdoor practical (lantern or low lamp) for evening frames. Shadows should be consistent with the sun angle visible through living/dining openings., japandi aesthetic, warm_neutral color palette, balanced furnishing, A calm, family-friendly Japandi scheme that balances Scandinavian warmth and Japanese restraint. Warm neutrals, low-profile furniture, woven textures and tactile ceramics create a serene backdrop. Prioritize negative space, natural materials, and a small dedicated work corner while keeping circulation and crib placement safe., Wide-to-medium frame showing covered patio as seamless extension of living/dining. Camera height ~0.9–1.0m; 24–35mm lens to read outdoor scale while preserving depth to interior openings. Visible light sources: natural daylight, one visible lantern/side lamp. Staging: low bench or outdoor lounge, small side table, one medium potted plant. Do not add furniture that contradicts outdoor scale. Image-pass recommendation: optional editorial pass only if the patio view appears washed; preserve exterior color and contrast., must include clear view to interior living/dining, low outdoor seating or bench, potted plant, low bench or outdoor lounge, clear sight-lines to living/dining, must avoid bright saturated colors or primary hues, high-gloss reflective surfaces, heavy patterned textiles or ornate decoration, over-accessorizing and clutter, lighting that originates from off-frame sources (no invisible light), placing indoor-scale furniture on patio, bright or clashing outdoor textiles, heavy outdoor furniture that reads indoor-scale, bright outdoor textiles that clash with interior palette, people, visible brand logos, architectural photography style, Canon 5D, 35mm lens, professional staging, high resolution, sharp focus, photo taken for a luxury property listing Create one photorealistic staged Relax still for a premium real-estate listing. Maintain believable residential scale for Relax at roughly medium. Use japandi styling, warm_neutral palette, soft_ambient lighting, and balanced furnishing density. A calm, family-friendly Japandi scheme that balances Scandinavian warmth and Japanese restraint. Warm neutrals, low-profile furniture, woven textures and tactile ceramics create a serene backdrop. Prioritize negative space, natural materials, and a small dedicated work corner while keeping circulation and crib placement safe. Shadows should be consistent with the sun angle visible through living/dining openings.
